summary: NullReferenceException in SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation when an exception thrown during SKSvgCanvas drawing is caught externally, leaving the canvas and managed stream undisposed so the native finalizer later invokes the write callback on a GC'd object.

stepsToReproduce: Create an SKSvgCanvas backed by a MemoryStream
Draw on the canvas via a method that throws an exception
Catch the exception externally without disposing the canvas first
Observe a NullReferenceException later in SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation

bugSignals.errorMessage: System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object. at SkiaSharp.DelegateProxies.SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The managed stream delegate proxy code has not changed and still lacks null-checks. The WeakReference-based GetUserData pattern can return null when the managed object is GC'd.

regression.reason: Reporter claims it worked in 2.88.9. The delegate proxy infrastructure was refactored in 3.x to use WeakReference-based user data, which may have changed the failure mode from a silent error to a crash.

analysis.summary: The root cause is a missing null-check in SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation. When an exception is thrown during canvas drawing and caught externally, the SKSvgCanvas and its owned SKManagedWStream are never disposed. Later, when Skia's native code (or the GC finalizer) tries to write to the stream via the delegate proxy, GetUserData returns null because the managed object's WeakReference target has been collected, causing NullReferenceException.
analysis.rationale: This is a real bug in the delegate proxy layer: SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation dereferences the result of GetUserData without a null-check. The reporter's code has a usage flaw (not using try/finally for disposal), but the library should handle this gracefully rather than crashing the entire Blazor app. The read-stream proxy (DelegateProxies.stream.cs) has the same pattern. Related issue #3520 also involves SKSvgCanvas and SKManagedWStream but describes a different symptom (missing closing SVG tag).

keySignals.text: Object reference not set to an instance of an object. at SkiaSharp.DelegateProxies.SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation
keySignals.interpretation: GetUserData returned null — the managed stream object was already GC'd or disposed when the native callback fired.

keySignals.text: if this throws an exception, i later get the exception mentioned above
keySignals.interpretation: Exception during drawing prevents canvas.Dispose() from running, leaving the managed stream in a dangling state for native callbacks.

keySignals.text: resulting in, the whole Blazor App crashes
keySignals.interpretation: Unhandled exception in UnmanagedCallersOnly callback is fatal — Blazor cannot catch it.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation calls GetUserData then immediately dereferences the result with stream.OnWrite() — no null-check. If the WeakReference target is null, this throws NullReferenceException.
GetUserData returns (T)weak.Target when the user data was created with makeWeak:true. WeakReference.Target returns null after GC collects the object, so GetUserData can return null.

CreateUserData is called with makeWeak:true, meaning the managed stream is held by a WeakReference. If nothing else holds a strong reference and the stream is not properly disposed, the GC can collect it.

SKSvgCanvas.Create(bounds, Stream) creates an SKManagedWStream and passes it to SKObject.Owned, meaning the canvas owns the stream. If the canvas is never disposed, the stream is never disposed either.
DisposeManaged iterates OwnedObjects and disposes them. This only runs when the canvas is explicitly disposed (disposing=true), not during finalization.

workarounds: Wrap the canvas in a using statement or try/finally block to ensure disposal even when exceptions occur.
nextQuestions: Should all delegate proxy implementations (wstream, stream, drawable, trace) add null-checks for GetUserData results?
Was the behavior different in 2.88.x because GetUserData did not use WeakReference?

resolution.hypothesis: The delegate proxy callbacks lack null-checks on the result of GetUserData. When the managed object is collected (WeakReference.Target returns null), the proxy crashes instead of returning a safe default.

proposals.title: Use try/finally to ensure canvas disposal
proposals.description: Wrap the canvas usage in a using statement or try/finally block so the canvas and its owned managed stream are always properly disposed, even when drawing methods throw exceptions.

proposals.title: Add null-checks in delegate proxy callbacks
proposals.description: Add null-checks to SKManagedWStreamWriteProxyImplementation and the other wstream proxy methods so they return safe defaults (false/0) instead of crashing when GetUserData returns null.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use try/finally to ensure canvas disposal
resolution.recommendedReason: This is a straightforward usage fix the reporter can apply immediately. The library-side null-check fix is also warranted but requires a code change and rebuild.

actionability.reason: The null-check gap in the delegate proxy is a real defensive programming issue that should be fixed, but the reporter's immediate problem is solvable with proper disposal. Needs investigation to determine if all proxy callbacks should be hardened.

actions.description: Apply bug, SkiaSharp, Windows, and SVG backend labels
